# aikido_container_config (Resource)

Manages the scanning configuration of an existing container repository in Aikido Security.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "aikido_containers" "all" {}

locals {
  my_container = one([for c in data.aikido_containers.all.containers : c if c.name == "my-app"])
}

resource "aikido_container_config" "my_app" {
  container_repo_id = local.my_container.id
  active            = true
  sensitivity       = "sensitive"
  internet_exposed  = "connected"
  tag_filter        = "v*"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `container_repo_id` (String) The ID of the container repository to manage.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether scanning is active for this container.
- `internet_exposed` (String) The internet exposure status: `connected`, `not_connected`, or `unknown`.
- `sensitivity` (String) The sensitivity level: `extreme`, `sensitive`, `normal`, `not_sensitive`, or `no_data`.
- `tag_filter` (String) Tag filter pattern for scanning. Supports wildcards (`*`) and `semver-production`. Empty string resets the filter.

### Read-Only

- `distro` (String) The OS distribution.
- `id` (String) The container repository ID.
- `name` (String) The name of the container repository.
- `provider_name` (String) The registry provider.
- `registry_name` (String) The name of the registry.
- `tag` (String) The current tag being scanned.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import a container configuration by its container repo ID
terraform import aikido_container_config.my_app "62637"
```
